new_classes.definition_en: Water, including mineral water and aerated water, to which sugar, other sweetening matter, or flavouring has been added, classified under CN subheading 2202 10 00 (Chapter 22). Includes soft drinks as described in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2202, paragraph (A). The presence of anti-oxidants, vitamins, stabilisers, or quinine does not affect classification.

The quality of having sugar, other sweetening matter, or flavouring added to a water-based beverage, distinguishing CN subheading 2202 10 00 (sweetened/flavoured water) from CN heading 2201 (plain water).

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria are well covered: (1) the water base (mineral, aerated, plain) is captured by subClassOf NonAlcoholicBeverage and the SweetenedWaterProcess restriction; (2) the presence of added sugar/sweetening matter/flavouring is captured by hasSweetenerOrFlavouring; (3) the CN code is pinned via codeString hasValue '22021000'; (4) the soft-drinks scope (HS Explanatory Note 2202(A)) is reflected in the class definition. A minor gap remains: the legal note specifies that anti-oxidants, vitamins, stabilisers, or quinine do NOT affect classification — this negative criterion is captured only in the textual definition and cannot be cleanly expressed as a positive OWL restriction without introducing individual named substances. The complement restriction against SparklingWine is a placeholder for the non-sparkling-wine disjointness already present in the broader TBox; full sibling disjointness is already encoded at the NonAlcoholicBeverage / Wine level.
